architecture.summary: N/A - No architectural changes. Three targeted fixes to existing modules: default scope constant, missing function call in init flow, guard removal in refresh flow.

integrations.summary: N/A - No external integrations. Internal CLI code changes affecting how plugins register in Claude Code settings.

ui-ux.summary: N/A - No direct UI. The fix ensures Claude Code Discover tab correctly shows all specweave plugins as installed (checkmark) by using user scope consistently.

performance.summary: N/A - No performance impact. Scope change is a config constant; enablePluginsInSettings is a simple JSON file write already performed in refresh-plugins.

security.summary: N/A - Scope change from project to user means plugins install to ~/.claude/settings.json instead of .claude/settings.json. Both are on the user's machine. No privilege escalation.

edge-cases.summary: Covered inline: (1) Existing projects with project-scoped plugins will get user-scoped on next refresh-plugins run. (2) The sw override in scopeOverrides becomes redundant since specweaveScope will now match it -- should be cleaned up. (3) enablePluginsInSettings is idempotent (sets key=true, harmless if already set).
